artifacts.title: Stomach ache
assertions: Sudden or severe abdominal pain and abdominal tenderness require immediate assessment.
Vomiting blood, bloody or black stool, inability to urinate, inability to pass stool or gas, breathing difficulty, chest pain, or collapse are emergency warning features.
Persistent or worsening pain, weight loss, pregnancy, urinary change, abnormal vaginal bleeding or discharge, persistent diarrhoea, and persistent bloating warrant timely clinical review.

artifacts.title: Ectopic pregnancy and miscarriage: diagnosis and initial management
assertions: Pregnancy-related abdominal or pelvic pain may occur with missed period, vaginal bleeding, dizziness, syncope, shoulder-tip pain, urinary symptoms, or gastrointestinal symptoms.
Pregnancy possibility should be considered in people of reproductive potential even when symptoms are non-specific.
Haemodynamic instability or significant concern about pain or bleeding requires direct emergency assessment.

artifacts.title: Suspected cancer: recognition and referral — abdominal symptoms
assertions: Persistent or unexplained abdominal pain requires age- and feature-dependent safety netting and investigation consideration.
Weight loss, rectal bleeding, persistent distension, early satiety, appetite loss, and altered bowel habit can modify investigation or referral decisions.
The interview records these features without assigning a cancer diagnosis.

artifacts.title: ACR Appropriateness Criteria: Acute Nonlocalized Abdominal Pain
assertions: Pain location, fever, recent surgery, and localized versus nonlocalized presentation affect downstream imaging consideration.
Questionnaire findings support clinician assessment but do not select or order imaging.

artifacts.title: STOM SNOMED CT MRCM allowed attributes for abdominal pain
assertions: Finding site and Severity were present as allowed attributes for the abdominal pain focus concept.
The MRCM result validates terminology modeling only and does not determine clinical questions, priority, or safety.
